title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXIX. Paradoxes.
content:
If a man who turnips cries
        Cries not when his father dies,
        It is a proof that he would rather
        Have a turnip than his father.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXIX. Paradoxes." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
